channel.title: Comments for page "Going In [REDACTED]"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Going In [REDACTED]"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh sorry, do you mean the report of the containment breach at the start? I do have an explanation in mind as to what happens and how, but much of it boils down to security guard alarm response SOPs, and those being fumbled in a specifically plot-convenient way. I didn't think it would be a good way to start the tale so it's only referred to from Bert's viewpoint, but all he knows is that they got told where to go and not for what.

Though if it might help for context.

Summary of Preceding Events
Summary of Preceding Events

SCP-1087 was going to be used in experiments that night. Upon entering the habitat the creature was unable to be located. Responding containment technician found damage to containment habitat indicating likely escape. Site security monitoring station was contacted, trainee-guard took details of call.

Now, their protocol at this time should be: Send first message with all information to the MTF, send second message for Site-wide general alert with censored files; so everyone is aware of MTF operating in area but not any classified info. (Yes, these SOPs are designed to fail in exactly the way that my story plays out.) What they do is: Draft up the Site-wide general alert, then include the responding MTF on the recipients list 'to save time by sending just one message'.

Their trainer, keeping less than a hawk-like eye on them, asks "So you notified the MTF and sent the Site-wide breach alert, right?". "Yep," the trainee responds. And the trainer doesn't find out what that actually means until he's getting yelled at by the MTF Captain.

At that point the MTF gets the proper files, acquires the proper equipment, and goes to meet up with the first responders.

Huh, I wonder if that should have been worked into the story somehow…?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, the part that left me feeling unsatisfied was the containment report. Like I feel like maybe the containment team was effected? But maybe not? I just feel like I missed something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi, thanks for reading! Your comment is understandable.

I'm afraid this tale does pretty much rely on the reader following the cross-link to the relevant SCP (near the end of the first section) in order to make the events transparent.

Spoiler-y excerpt and explanation
Spoiler-y excerpt and explanation

For the record, that relevant SCP is 1087.

From its Description:

SCP-1087's saliva contains an unknown chemical […] once it makes contact with a mammalian bloodstream, it travels to the subject's exocrine glands and alters their biochemical functions. This results in affected subjects producing and secreting a pheromone designated as SCP-1087-1.

Mammals coming into contact with SCP-1087-1 become increasingly agitated, specifically towards the being emitting the pheromone. As time passes, the concentration of pheromones will severely hamper the judgment of nearby animals, including humans, compelling all mammals near the affected subject to hunt and attack the organism emitting SCP-1087-1.

This pretty well sums up what took place that night (with the lost camper as the 'affected subject').

One suggestion I received was to present some of that (unredacted) description itself right in the tale, perhaps at the line break in between the two sections, but ultimately I decided not to. I wonder now if maybe I should try that, or if there's possibly a better way to present the crosslink.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm left feeling unsatisfied and like I missed something important. Like something is off but I can't identity what.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it. Not too much here to be said; the writing in the earlier sections didn't sit quite right with me, but it picks up later when they begin to really get in the thick of it, oddly enough. I was just reading in the discussion section of another tale earlier today about the importance of character placement (and, incidentally, how the article in question lacked it.) This has that; I never felt question about where anybody stood in my mental depiction of the events as they occurred. This could be resultant of the fact that a good chunk of it is Bert sitting in his truck stewing in a pit of nerves and anguish (very much enjoyed that bit) but there's still a good bit of movement later on when the reinforcements come on scene.

Overall - it does what it sets out to do well, and what it set out to do fit within my niche quite nicely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember reading this as a draft a while back. Some interesting worldbuilding and understated horror. Nicely done.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I feel like I've stalled out on working this draft, so here's posting it and hoping enough people like it well enough.

Thanks to everyone who took the time to put eyes on this draft for me!
Thanks to everyone who took the time to put eyes on this draft for me!

LadyKatie (forums)
Pecan (chat)
Nekronomykon (chat)
Cyantreuse (chat)
Hippo (chat)
Chubert (forums)
taylor_itkin (chat)
Modern_Erasmus (chat)

And anyone else I might have failed to include here.

E: For the record, the image for the map is a picture that was taken by me.
